# QD OLED vs OLED: What’s the difference?

When discussing different types of monitor panels, it’s easy to get lost in the sea of options. However, one of the latest panel technologies that is gaining a lot of notoriety is OLED panels. Whether it be OLED or QD OLED, these panels differentiate themselves by having no black light and an infinite contrast ratio, among many other distinguishing features. However, you might be wondering… what is the difference between OLED and QD OLED? And which one is better? We will explore exactly that in this article.

When discussing different types of monitor panels, it’s easy to get lost in the sea of options. However, one of the latest panel technologies that is gaining a lot of notoriety is OLED panels. Whether it be OLED or QD OLED, these panels differentiate themselves by having no black light and an infinite contrast ratio, among many other distinguishing features. However, you might be wondering… what is the difference between OLED and QD OLED? And which one is better? We will explore exactly that in this article.

Reading time: **2 Min**

![OLED vs QD OLED layers](/c-dam/b2c/category-pages/sound-and-vision/monitors/monitors-articles/qd-oled-vs-oled/oled-vs-qd-oled-banner.jpg)

## What is the difference between OLED and QD-OLED?

 

The main difference between OLED and QD OLED is that QD OLED uses quantum dot technology and therefore differs in pixel structure, which in turn creates even higher brightness and better HDR.

Although both technologies are state of the art, you can think of QD OLED as an enhancement of OLED. While OLED displays offer perfect blacks, infinite contrast ratios, and wide viewing angles, QD OLED offers enhanced color accuracy, better brightness, and better energy efficiency.

 

The main difference between OLED and QD OLED is that QD OLED uses quantum dot technology and therefore differs in pixel structure, which in turn creates even higher brightness and better HDR.

Although both technologies are state of the art, you can think of QD OLED as an enhancement of OLED. While OLED displays offer perfect blacks, infinite contrast ratios, and wide viewing angles, QD OLED offers enhanced color accuracy, better brightness, and better energy efficiency.

## **Is QD-OLED better than OLED?**

 

Based on stating the differences between these two technologies above, it is safe say that QD OLED is indeed an enhancement of OLED technology. Therefore, it is safe to say that QD OLED has a bit of an advantage on OLED panels. However, with that being said, it does not mean that OLED is not a superior technology. It has been on the market longer than OLED and is well known for its perfect blacks and excellent picture quality.

Therefore, it is safe to say that QD OLED has a bit of an advantage on OLED panels. However, with that being said, it does not mean that OLED is not a superior technology. It has been on the market longer than OLED and is well known for its perfect blacks and excellent picture quality.

## **What are the benefits of QD-OLED?**

 

There are many benefits to QD OLED, however, what makes QD OLED shine is the picture quality that it produces. OD OLED panels are known for enhanced color accuracy, incredible peak brightness, better energy efficiency, improved viewing angles, perfect black and infinite contrast ratios. So, when you combine all these benefits into one panel, QD OLED produces a color-filled experience that is hard to compete with.

OD OLED panels are known for enhanced color accuracy, incredible peak brightness, better energy efficiency, improved viewing angles, perfect black and infinite contrast ratios. So, when you combine all these benefits into one panel, QD OLED produces a color-filled experience that is hard to compete with.

## **Is a QD-OLED monitor worth it?**

 

If color vibrancy, high brightness, and deep contrast are your top criterion when looking for a monitor, then QD OLED is definitely worth it and should be your top choice for a monitor panel. When it is combined with other powerful features such as a high refresh rate and a high resolution, then it becomes a true asset to an incredible gaming experience.

An example of a monitor that combines multiple powerful specs in one is the Philips Evnia [32M2N8900](https://www.philips.co.uk/c-p/32M2N8900_00/4k-uhd-gaming-monitor-qd-oled-gaming-monitor). Its QD OLED panel, when combined with a 240Hz refresh rate and UHD 4K resolution, this monitor packs a powerful color punch that truly enhances your gaming experience.

# What is QD-OLED? 8 Popular QD-OLED Models to Consider in 2025

![QD-OLED | Vorson Digital](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHdpZHRoPSIxMDgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjUwMCIgdmlld0JveD0iMCAwIDEwODAgNTAwIj48cmVjdCB3aWR0aD0iMTAwJSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxMDAlIiBzdHlsZT0iZmlsbDojY2ZkNGRiO2ZpbGwtb3BhY2l0eTogMC4xOyIvPjwvc3ZnPg==)

Quantum Dot Organic Light-Emitting Diode (QD-OLED) is one of the most major developments in display technology in the last few years. In the year 2025, if you’re looking for a high-end TV or monitor, you’ll likely come across the word “QD-OLED”.

With its very accurate colors, deep blacks, and higher brightness, QD-OLED is changing the way we see things, whether we’re watching, gaming, or working. But what does QD-OLED mean, and which types should you think about? We’ll talk about the basics of QD-OLED technology and eight of the best QD-OLED models worth looking at in 2025 in this blog.

## **What is QD-OLED?**

The word QD-OLED comes from its acronym, which stands for Quantum Dot Organic Light-Emitting Diode. This is a new and advanced display technology that combines the best parts of OLED and Quantum Dots to produce better picture quality. Traditional OLED panels use color filters and white OLED subpixels to make colors look good. Whereas, QD-OLED panels use a blue OLED light source and Quantum Dots to make colors look more vibrant and pure. This makes pictures brighter, more vivid, and close to color and make QD-OLED a great choice for people who want high-quality visuals. It is increasingly being used in premium display setups, including [LED video walls](https://www.vorsondigital.com/product/smd-led-screens/), where exceptional clarity and color accuracy are essential.

### **How is QD-OLED Different from Traditional OLED?**

#### **Brighter and Bold Colors**

Most OLEDs use white light with color filters that soak up some light and make the screen less bright. However, QD-OLED technology uses Quantum Dots to turn blue light straight into red and green, so that there is no need for color filters. With a much wider color range, images look more real, accurate, and bright, which makes them great for HDR material and professional use.

#### **Better Contrast and Deep Blacks**

Additionally, QD-OLED panels have self-emissive pixels, which means that each pixel can turn off fully to create true black levels. Through making an infinite contrast ratio, dark scenes in games and movies look incredibly real and full of detail.

#### **Reduced Burn-in Issues**

Burn-in happens when static pictures leave permanent marks over time. It was always a problem with OLED technology. . Blue OLED, which is used in QD-OLED screens, is more resistant to degradation than white OLED and makes them more durable and long-lasting.

#### **Enhanced Energy Efficiency**

QD-OLED displays are more energy-efficient than regular OLEDs because they need less power to achieve the same level of brightness after removing poor color filters. This is advantageous for both eco conscious users and those trying to cut electricity bills.

This technology is becoming very important in the advanced entertainment and business markets because big companies like Samsung, Sony, Alienware, and LG are using it in their premium displays.

## **How QD-OLED Works**

There are three layers that make QD-OLED screens work:

### **Blue OLED Light Source**

A blue OLED layer that emits light on its own is at the base of QD-OLED technology and is the main source of light. QD-OLED screens only use blue light as their main source, while regular OLED displays use separate red, green, and blue sub-pixels. This method not only makes the structure of the display easier, but it also makes it more durable by preventing gradual color fading. The blue OLED layer makes sure that the light output is even and stable, which is essential for a high-performance display.

### **Quantum Dots (QD) Layer**

A carefully designed Quantum Dot (QD) layer changes some of the blue light into red and green. This makes a true RGB color range that doesn’t need any regular color filters. Quantum Dots are tiny semiconductor particles that are measured in nanometers and give off very pure and vibrant hues when they are excited by light. The conversion process gives accurate colors, wide color range , and overall high brightness. As this method removes wasteful parts of color filters, it decreases energy use and shows performance much better.

### **Final Display Panel**

The display has deeper blacks, more vibrant colors, and better light than regular OLED screens because of this creative mix. These QD-OLED screens have higher luminance levels, better HDR performance, and use less power because they don’t need color filters and instead use the natural power of Quantum Dots. It also lets you have almost infinite contrast ratios because the technology is self-emitting. This makes the watching experience more alive and real.

QD-OLED technology is the next step forward in high-end display performance, whether you use it for gaming, making professional material, or watching movies.

## **Benefits of QD-OLED Over Other Display Technologies**

### **1. Good Color Accuracy**

QD-OLED displays have 100% DCI-P3 coverage, which means they can show the exact colors that the people who made the material wanted to see. They are great for photographers, video editors, computer artists, and anyone else who wants images that look like real life.

### **2. High Brightness**

Some of the biggest problems with regular OLEDs can be fixed with Quantum Dot technology, which makes QD-OLED screens brighter at their brightest.

### **3. Deep Blacks and Infinite Contrast**

QD-OLED technology features self-emissive pixels that can switch off individually, achieving perfect black levels and infinite contrast. This enhances image depth, sharpens details in dark scenes, and creates a more immersive, true-to-life viewing experience.

### **4. Wide Viewing Angles**

Traditional LED screens lose their color accuracy at very high or very low angles, but QD-OLED displays keep their color accuracy and brightness all the way through. This makes for a better viewing experience, even when looking at it from the side. There won’t be any color change or washout that you can see.

### **5. Fast Response Time for Gamers**

Nearly immediate pixel reaction times allow QD-OLED technology to remove ghosting and motion blur. Perfect for fast-paced competitive gaming and high-refresh-rate screens, gamers gain from ultra-smooth images, decreased input lag, and accurate action sequences.

## **8 Best QD-OLED Models to Consider in 2025**

### **1. Samsung S95D QD-OLED TV**

The best 4K QD-OLED TV from Samsung has AI-powered upscaling, a 120Hz refresh rate, and HDR10+ support. It’s the best home entertainment TV you can get. Ultra-vivid colors, deep blacks, and smooth motion are what you can expect. This is great for movie fans and gamers who want the best viewing experience possible.

### **2. Sony A95L QD-OLED TV**

Sony’s A95L is all about making excellent movies. It has amazing clarity, contrast, and color accuracy due to IMAX-enhanced technology, top-notch motion control, and the powerful Cognitive Processor XR. Every scene feels more real, whether you’re watching a big movie or a high-action sports game.

### **3. Alienware AW3425DW QD-OLED Monitor**

This 34-inch ultra-wide monitor is made for avid players. It has a resolution of 3440×1440, a refresh rate of 175Hz, and NVIDIA G-Sync to make gameplay smooth. Its curvy shape pulls you into the action, and QD-OLED technology makes sure that the colors and contrast are stunning.

### **4. Samsung Odyssey G9 QD-OLED Monitor**

With a 240Hz refresh rate and an ultra-wide 5120×1440 resolution, the 49-inch Samsung Odyssey G9 is the best game monitor ever. You can expect clear details, very smooth movements, and a fully immersive, surround-sound experience whether you’re roaming open worlds or playing fast moving esports.

### **5. LG UltraGear 32QD95 QD-OLED Monitor**

This 32-inch 4K monitor was made for creative professionals and has great color accuracy, HDR support, and smooth operation. Every detail is shown with accuracy and true-to-life color when you’re editing high-resolution pictures, making designs and videos.

### **6. Philips Momentum 559M1RYV QD-OLED TV**

This 55-inch screen is a console gamer’s dream. It has Ambilight technology for a more realistic gaming experience and Dolby Vision for amazing visuals. It’s great for PlayStation and Xbox fans because it has very little input delay and smooth motion.

### **7. Asus ROG Swift PG42UQ QD-OLED Monitor**

The best gaming experience ever. This 42-inch QD-OLED monitor has a high frame rate, NVIDIA G-Sync, and AMD FreeSync for smooth, quick performance. This monitor makes sure that motion is smooth and graphics are stunning, whether you like competitive games or deep single-player adventures.

### **8. TCL X10 QD-OLED TV**

The TCL X10 is great for people who want the highest level views without paying high prices. It’s a great choice for buyers on a budget who still want a great home entertainment experience because of its 4K resolution, bright QD-OLED colors, and HDR support.

## **QD-OLED Solutions at Vorson Digital**

QD-OLED technology is being used by Vorson Digital, a leader in custom digital display solutions, to make the latest digital signs solutions. These things are good for businesses:

### **Unparalleled Image Quality**

QD-OLED screens show images that are very clear and have a lot of contrast, with dark blacks and bright colors. Digital signage stands out because of its excellent resolution, which grabs customers’ attention and improves brand messaging in busy business areas.

### **Customizable Display Solutions**

Vorson Digital has custom QD-OLED signage systems that are made to fit the needs of retail stores, corporate offices, and advertising campaigns. To get people most interested, businesses can pick from different sizes, forms, and interactive features.

### **Advanced Durability**

Customized for long-term business use, QD-OLED displays offer longer life, lower risks of burn-in, and better energy economy. For reliable performance in busy places like malls, airports, and business centers, these qualities are necessary.

### **Professional Installation & Support**

Expert installation, calibration, and continued maintenance for QD-OLED displays are all done by Vorson Digital. This makes sure that the system works at its best, delays interruptions, and lets businesses focus on their customers while we take care of technical help and system updates.

Vorson Digital’s QD-OLED products provide an advanced visual experience for companies that want to improve their brand recognition and connect with customers more effectively.

## **Conclusion**

QD-OLED is changing the display industry because it has the best color accuracy, brightness, contrast, and energy economy. Buying a QD-OLED display in 2025 is the best way to experience the best visual technology, whether you’re a fan of entertainment, a skilled creator, or the owner of a business.

Vorson Digital’s QD-OLED solutions offer the best visual quality of any modern [digital signs](https://www.vorsondigital.com/), making sure your company shines in a market that is becoming more and more competitive.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: QD-OLED technology allows each pixel to turn off completely, achieving true black levels and an infinite contrast ratio, resulting in unparalleled depth and detail in dark scenes.
Utilizing quantum dots, QD-OLED displays produce vibrant colors with high accuracy and a broader color spectrum, improving overall image quality and making visuals more lifelike.

QD-OLED combines self-emissive pixel technology with quantum dots, offering superior brightness, color accuracy, and energy efficiency, giving it a significant advantage over traditional OLED displays.
